photometry.remarks: [Last non detection] Data provided by ZTF

payload.uvot_just: We wish to map the entire transient SED in all UV filters.
payload.science_just: This request is for ZTF25aaktqzg which Swift has obtained a UV light curve for. We request a single epoch template image in order to perform difference imaging for an upcoming publication.
payload.exp_time_just: These observations will provide a deep enough template image for subtraction of host galaxy light.
payload.immediate_objective: We wish to obtain template imaging in order to finalize supernova photometry for publication.

Typically, the Recombination wave causes the r-band plateau to stay flat and the g-band to decline steadily. This could be due to additional contribution from 56Ni-mixed or even CSM under the photosphere. The transition phase would give us more inputs as to what's going on.
SN II starts fading after 90d rise
@ragnhild this is pretty weird. It is also getting brighter in J
Is it normal that the r-band luminosity keeps steadily rising on the plateau like that?
Super-red plateau at 70d (g-r=1 mag)

At first glance also looks like a post-peak Ia to me, but this one is still rising.. And the narrow emission line at 6680 Ang seems real (also in the 2D spectrum), which, if Halpha, would be at z=0.0175?
good match to Ia with re-reduced spectrum @jesper
VERY nice reduction @ptgcliu . I think it is a Ic
I re-reduced the NOT spectrum with the host galaxy subtraction pipeline I developed. Preliminary results uploaded. Surprisingly evident broad Ca ii and maybe Ti trough. @jesper @steve-schulze
blue side matches to Ic-BL and Ia-pec. high velocity O or Ca II not present, more likely a Ia? @jesper @fremling
Something is wrong with the NGPS reduction @fremling @frank-verdi - the strong host Na D line seen in KAST is absent in NGPS; possibly wavelength calibration error?
Matches to SN Ic-BL with significant extinction. ZTF light curves indicates phase of around peak-light. Redshift from SNID template matching.
